workflow.name: Synthetic DevHub status review
workflow.purpose: Observe one attended, read-only status-review path after the user has completed any required sign-in outside automation.
workflow.starting_condition: User is present and has manually navigated to a DevHub page that lists their own permit or application records.
workflow.ending_condition: Observer records only redacted structure, status labels, and navigation outcomes needed to update PP&D guardrails.

required_before_observation: Confirm the user is present for the entire authenticated DevHub observation.
Confirm login, MFA, CAPTCHA, and account recovery are performed manually by the user only.
Confirm the observer will not store cookies, credentials, browser storage, screenshots, traces, HAR files, downloaded documents, or raw authenticated page values.
Confirm the selected workflow is status review only and does not include upload, payment, scheduling, certification, cancellation, or submission.
Confirm the observer may stop immediately if the page exposes sensitive values that cannot be safely redacted.
manual_user_actions_only: Selecting an account-owned permit or application when private records are visible
Opening any document, message, invoice, correction item, payment screen, upload control, or submission review screen

read_only_actions_to_map.notes: Record only labels and structural roles. Do not record real permit numbers, project names, addresses, or account identifiers.
Record control labels and whether the control appears reversible. Do not enter search terms from the user's private records.
The user controls navigation into and out of any record detail page. Observer records only redacted headings, tabs, and status labels.

allowed_observations: Page heading text when it does not contain private case data
Generic status labels such as Submitted, In Review, Corrections Requested, Issued, Closed, or Expired
Presence or absence of read-only navigation controls
Presence or absence of upload, payment, schedule, cancel, certify, or submit controls as blocked consequential surfaces
Validation or alert message templates after private values have been removed
allowed_automation: No browser automation in this fixture packet
Future live runs may use attended read-only observation only after this fixture validates
blocked_actions: Paying fees or entering payment details
Certifying, acknowledging, submitting, withdrawing, cancelling, or otherwise changing an official record

storage_rules: Do not commit screenshots, videos, traces, HAR files, storage state, downloaded documents, or raw authenticated HTML.
Do not persist exact private field values even when they look procedural.
Store only synthetic examples and redacted structural summaries in fixtures.

completion_evidence: Fixture packet validated before any live observation.
No private DevHub session files or raw authenticated artifacts were created.
Any future live observation journal contains only redacted metadata and blocked-action notes.

example_commit_safe_journal.accessible_structure_summary: Observed dashboard-style navigation, status list/table labels, generic status text, and detail-view headings with private values redacted.
guardrail_followups: Classify upload, payment, scheduling, certification, cancellation, and submission controls as consequential action gates.
Keep status review available as attended read-only observation only.
example_commit_safe_journal.completion_evidence: Synthetic packet only; no live session data captured.
